text.source_text: यथा मनुष्याधिकारनियममपोद्य देवादीनामपि विद्यास्वधिकार उक्तः , तथैव द्विजात्यधिकारनियमापवादेन शूद्रस्याप्यधिकारः स्यादित्येतामाशङ्कां निवर्तयितुमिदमधिकरणमारभ्यते । तत्र शूद्रस्या प्यधिकारः स्यादिति तावत्प्राप्तम् ; अर्थित्वसामर्थ्ययोः सम्भवात् , ‘ तस्माच्छूद्रो यज्ञेऽनवकॢप्तः ’ (तै. सं. ७ । १ । १ । ६) इतिवत् ‘ शूद्रो विद्यायामनवकॢप्त ’ इति निषेधाश्रवणात् । यच्च कर्मस्वनधिकारकारणं शूद्रस्यानग्नित्वम् , न तद्विद्यास्वधिकारस्यापवादकं लिङ्गम् । न ह्याहवनीयादिरहितेन विद्या वेदितुं न शक्यते । भवति च श्रौतं लिङ्गं शूद्राधिकारस्योपोद्बलकम् । संवर्गविद्यायां हि जानश्रुतिं पौत्रायणं शुश्रूषुं शूद्रशब्देन परामृशति — ‘ अह हारे त्वा शूद्र तवैव सह गोभिरस्तु ’ (छा. उ. ४ । २ । ३) इति । विदुरप्रभृतयश्च शूद्रयोनिप्रभवा अपि विशिष्टविज्ञानसम्पन्नाः स्मर्यन्ते । तस्मा दधिक्रियते शूद्रो विद्यास्वित्येवं प्राप्ते ब्रूमः —
text.transliteration_iast: yathā manuṣyādhikāraniyamamapodya devādīnāmapi vidyāsvadhikāra uktaḥ , tathaiva dvijātyadhikāraniyamāpavādena śūdrasyāpyadhikāraḥ syādityetāmāśaṅkāṃ nivartayitumidamadhikaraṇamārabhyate | tatra śūdrasyā pyadhikāraḥ syāditi tāvatprāptam ; arthitvasāmarthyayoḥ sambhavāt , ‘ tasmācchūdro yajñe'navakḷptaḥ ’ (tai. saṃ. 7 | 1 | 1 | 6) itivat ‘ śūdro vidyāyāmanavakḷpta ’ iti niṣedhāśravaṇāt | yacca karmasvanadhikārakāraṇaṃ śūdrasyānagnitvam , na tadvidyāsvadhikārasyāpavādakaṃ liṅgam | na hyāhavanīyādirahitena vidyā vedituṃ na śakyate | bhavati ca śrautaṃ liṅgaṃ śūdrādhikārasyopodbalakam | saṃvargavidyāyāṃ hi jānaśrutiṃ pautrāyaṇaṃ śuśrūṣuṃ śūdraśabdena parāmṛśati — ‘ aha hāre tvā śūdra tavaiva saha gobhirastu ’ (chā. u. 4 | 2 | 3) iti | viduraprabhṛtayaśca śūdrayoniprabhavā api viśiṣṭavijñānasampannāḥ smaryante | tasmā dadhikriyate śūdro vidyāsvityevaṃ prāpte brūmaḥ —

translation.literal_translation: Just as the rule limiting eligibility to humans was set aside and gods were granted eligibility in vidya, one might similarly suspect that by setting aside a twice-born-only rule even the Shudra would be eligible. To remove that doubt this adhikarana begins. At first glance, Shudra eligibility seems arguable: desire and capacity may be present; and while there is an explicit prohibition like 'therefore the Shudra is not fit for sacrifice,' no similarly explicit prohibition for vidya is heard. The reason for ineligibility in karma - lack of sacred fires - does not by itself negate eligibility in vidya, since knowing vidya does not require ahavaniya etc. There also appears to be scriptural support: in Samvarga-vidya Janashruti Pautrayana seeking instruction is addressed with the word 'Shudra.' And figures like Vidura, though born in Shudra lineage, are remembered as possessing exceptional knowledge. Therefore one might conclude: the Shudra is eligible for vidyas. In response we say -
translation.technical_translation: Opening of 1.3.34 frames prima facie argument for shudra-adhikara in brahma-vidya from desire/capacity and selected textual indicators, preparing formal refutation.
translation.interpretive_note: Sets up the next adhikarana through a strong purvapaksha staging.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit adhikara and varna terminology choices in records 1.3.086-1.3.088.
